mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate accelerates by more than 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS decline × HOUST decline) shows no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight capacity utilization, a rise in the industrial production index signals positive supply-side momentum that eases inflationary pressures on margins, boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO rises during high-capacity utilization regimes compared to baseline periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate earnings revisions, which mechanically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets price in stronger aggregate demand.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital compresses equity risk premiums and valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of nonfarm payrolls and core inflation indicates an overheating economy that forces aggressive monetary tightening, ultimately depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sharp rebound in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not significantly outperform during HOUST rebounds in post-shock recovery periods compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
